observed: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is an older canonical snapshot than later additive queue-update commits.
M-012 completed a non-authoritative queue-state materializer and handed off explicit manifest binding.
inferred: Without a path-and-byte manifest, a caller can supply a selective additive update set while still producing a structurally valid materialized queue.
proposed: Use a canonical SHA-256 manifest over safe queue-update paths, file digests, and unique event orders before materialization.
superseded: Treating an unbound explicit update list as sufficient evidence of complete queue history.
unresolved: Complete independent enumeration of operations/queue-updates was not available in this run.
A structurally valid enumeration attestation does not prove the enumerator was independent or truthful.

verification.route: isolated Node.js test run against byte-equivalent source before commit
covered: unsafe path and malformed digest rejection

privacy_boundary: Public repository paths, hashes, commit identifiers, operational claim states, and abstracted continuity conclusions only. No raw chat, health, credentials, identifying personal data, or unreviewed uploads.
falsification_route: Re-run the committed tests; mutate a bound path, digest, or event order and verify rejection or digest change. Reject authority if complete tree enumeration cannot be independently reproduced.
